meta.pageTitle: Title 3 — Real Property Tax · govOS · Kilo Aupuni
meta.eyebrow: 12 Stones Global · Kilo Aupuni · govOS · public service

hero.lead: A plain-language navigator to Maui County Code Title 3, Ch. 3.48 (Real Property Tax): a live parcel lookup, the tax-year calendar, the $300,000 home exemption, how property is classified, how to appeal your assessment, and who pays. Assessment is by the Real Property Assessment Division (Finance); you pay at mauipropertytax.com — this service explains the law and hands you off to the official systems, it does not replace them. Rates change every year; figures marked expanding are being filled in. Nothing here is legal or tax advice.

sections.title: ★ Parcel lookup
sections.note: Your tax starts with the parcel. Enter a Maui County TMK to see its designations from the Hawaiʻi Statewide GIS. For the parcel's assessed value, classification, exemptions, and tax bill (held by the Real Property Assessment Division, not GIS), the result links you to the qPublic assessment record and the mauipropertytax.com payment portal.

source.html: Live designations: Hawaiʻi Statewide GIS. Assessment record: qPublic — Maui Real Property Assessment; pay at mauipropertytax.com. No parcel or tax data is stored or fabricated — fetched live / linked to source.

sections.title: 1 · The property-tax year
sections.note: The annual cycle under Ch. 3.48 — assessment, appeal, then two payment installments.

steps.name: Assessment notice — by March 15
steps.what: The Real Property Assessment Division mails your annual assessment notice by March 15, showing assessed value and classification for the coming tax year.
steps.pay: Expense: none to receive · the County assesses

steps.name: Appeal window — by April 9
steps.what: If you disagree, file an appeal with the Board of Review by April 9, with a $75 fee per appeal. You must still pay your taxes on time while the appeal is pending.
steps.pay: Expense: $75 appeal fee · paid by: owner

steps.name: 1st installment — due August 20
steps.what: The first-half bill is mailed July 20 and is due August 20.
steps.pay: Expense: half the year's tax · paid by: owner

steps.name: 2nd installment — due February 20
steps.what: The second-half bill is mailed January 20 and is due February 20.
steps.pay: Expense: half the year's tax · paid by: owner

steps.name: Late: 10% + 1%/month
steps.what: Miss a due date and a one-time 10% penalty plus 1% monthly interest accrues on the unpaid balance until paid.
steps.pay: Expense: penalty + interest · paid by: owner

position.label: ⚖️ Position — James Langford (analysis, not law; a stance beside the rule)
position.text: Real property tax is the County taxing a resident's own home and land. The home exemption and owner-occupied class exist to protect the homestead — reform (the Council's active RPT-reform work) should keep lightening the owner-occupant relative to investor, STR, and resort classes, not the reverse.

source.html: County of Maui Finance — Real Property Tax Payments + Appeals; dates per County notices (assessments by Mar 15, appeal by Apr 9). MCC Ch. 3.48.

sections.title: 2 · Key dates & amounts
sections.note: The fixed dates and amounts of the RPT year. Tax RATES (per $1,000 of value, by class) are set by the Council annually — see the County rates page.

rows.v: $75 fee per appeal; cannot be appealed late
for the following assessment year; missing it cannot be appealed

source.html: County of Maui Finance — RPT Payments, Tax Rates, Exemptions FAQ. Amounts as published; confirm the current year with Finance.

sections.title: 3 · The home exemption — $300,000 off
sections.note: The single biggest break for resident homeowners. File once; it carries forward while you qualify.
sections.html: The home exemption reduces your taxable assessed value by $300,000 and reclassifies the property into the lower-taxed Owner-occupied class. To qualify you must: occupy the home more than 270 days of each calendar year; not rent the entire premises for any part of the year; and file a Hawaiʻi resident income-tax return with a Maui County address for the prior year. File your claim by December 31 for it to apply to the following assessment year — and note that missing the December 31 deadline cannot be appealed.

source.html: Maui County Code Ch. 3.48, Art. IX (Exemptions) — §3.48.455 + County Exemptions FAQ.

sections.title: 4 · Tax classifications
sections.note: Your classification (not just your value) drives your rate. The Council sets a separate rate for each class.

rows.v: Principal home with a granted home exemption (lowest tier)
Dwelling that isn't owner-occupied or another specific class
Leased 12+ months (reduced rate to encourage resident housing)
Transient vacation rental / short-term rental home

source.html: County of Maui — Classification for Tax-Rate Purposes + MCC Ch. 3.48 Art. IX (§3.48.410).
source.expanding: full class list + the year's per-class rates

sections.title: 5 · Appeals
sections.note: If your assessed value is wrong, you can challenge it — but on the County's clock. Not tax advice.
sections.html: File an appeal with the Board of Review by April 9 (the next business day if it falls on a weekend/holiday), with a $75 fee per appeal. Common grounds: the assessment exceeds market value, lacks uniformity with comparable properties, or the classification/exemption is wrong. You must keep paying your taxes by the Aug 20 / Feb 20 due dates while the appeal is pending — an appeal does not pause the bill, and unpaid amounts still draw the 10% + 1%/month penalty.

source.html: County of Maui — Appeals + Appeal Instructions & Form.
source.expanding: Board of Review vs. Tax Appeal Court track

sections.title: 6 · Who pays — the charter lens
sections.note: Labeled analysis — the tax read through two charters. Not adopted law; an interpretive lens beside the Code.
sections.mauiCharter: Under the Maui County Charter, the Council sets real-property tax rates by class each year and Finance assesses and collects; RPT is the County's primary own-source revenue funding county services. The Charter frames RPT as the Council's central fiscal lever — and the active Real Property Tax Reform (RPTR) committee is where class definitions and rates are being reconsidered.
sections.sovereignCharter: Under the Sovereign Charter (12 Stones) lens, a recurring tax on one's own homestead is the heaviest ongoing claim the County makes on a resident. The home exemption and owner-occupied class are the homestead protections; the lens reads reform as a duty to shift the load toward investor / STR / resort classes and away from the resident owner-occupant and long-term-rental housing.

position.label: ⚖️ Position — analysis, not law
position.text: Classification is the moral core of RPT: it decides who carries the County. The reform question — read through both charters — is whether each new rate lightens the resident homeowner and long-term housing, or quietly leans on them. Same tax, read two ways.

sections.title: 7 · Reform & codification candidates
sections.intro: Provisions that, in our assessment, the Council's RPT-reform work should codify or index into Ch. 3.48 so they don't drift with administration or inflation. Citations are verbatim-checked; the recommendation is a stance beside the rule.
items: Index the home exemption. The $300,000 exemption is a fixed dollar amount that erodes as values rise. → Codify an inflation index so the homestead protection holds its real value. expanding

Codify classification criteria. Class placement (e.g. long-term-rental vs non-owner-occupied) drives the bill but turns on administrative definitions. → Tighten the in-Code criteria so classification is predictable and appealable.

Rate-setting transparency. Per-class rates are set annually. → Require a published, plain-language who-pays-what table with each rate ordinance so residents can see the shift between classes year over year.

source.html: Analysis grounded in MCC Ch. 3.48 + the County's classification framework; reform context = the Council's Real Property Tax Reform committee.

sections.text: Free now (no account): this page, the live parcel lookup, the tax-year calendar, the dates/amounts table, and the classification list are all Tier 1 — free. Coming — Tier 3 (verified tools): a home-exemption eligibility checker, an appeal-prep helper, and an AI property-tax assistant will be verification-gated (identity-verified). Identity is verified through the payment processor — only verified-status is stored here, never the raw ID. No live billing is enabled (Stripe keys are environment references only); the actual tax payment always happens on the County's own portal, mauipropertytax.com.
